content: read reviews/43.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/43.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/43.md.

1→# Review: deepmind/43.lean
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

     8→## 2. Import Statement

    10→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).

    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring

    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    16→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 43`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/43`.
    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
    19→- [x] Bibliography entry present: [Er95] with full citation.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos43 ... end Erdos43`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos43`).
    25→- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 32).
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `IsSidonSet` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    30→- [x] `DisjointDifferences` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    31→- N/A: No noncomputable operations (pure logical predicates).
    32→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    33→- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSidonSet`, `DisjointDifferences`.
    34→- N/A: No functions returning types to check for `lowerCamelCase`.
    35→- N/A: No problem-specific `local notation` needed.
    36→- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks added (optional "consider" item).
    37→
    38→## 6. Theorem Statements
    39→
    40→- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
    41→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_43` (snake_case, matching namespace number 43).
    42→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions.
    43→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
    44→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    45→- [x] Docstring references the source paper: [Er95].
    46→
    47→### `answer()` usage
    48→
    49→The original problem asks "is it true that ..." — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    50→
    51→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked.
    52→- [x] Problem is open, so `answer(sorry)` (not `answer(True)`/`answer(False)`) is correct.
    53→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: `∃ C : ℕ, ∀ (N : ℕ) (A B S : Finset ℕ), [Sidon & range conditions] → [S is maximal] → DisjointDifferences A B → Nat.choose A.card 2 + Nat.choose B.card 2 ≤ Nat.choose S.card 2 + C`. Thus `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no".
    54→- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ C`, `∀ N A B S`) go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.
    55→
    56→## 7. Category Attribute
    57→
    58→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    59→- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
    60→- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body — consistent.
    61→
    62→## 8. AMS Attribute
    63→
    64→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present: `AMS 5 11`.
    65→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 11).
    66→- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5 < 11.
    67→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    68→- [x] Both `5` (combinatorics) and `11` (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    69→
    70→## 9. Proof Bodies
    71→
    72→- [x] Open conjecture uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (lines 68–69), which is acceptable.
    73→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    74→- N/A: File is not in `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
    75→- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
    76→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    77→
    78→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    79→
    80→- N/A: No variants present. Only the main theorem is declared.
    81→- **Note:** The source problem on erdosproblems.com includes a second part asking whether the bound can be improved when |A| = |B|. This second part is not formalized. A variant `erdos_43.variants.equal_size` could be added but is not required.
    82→
    83→## 11. Code Quality
    84→
    85→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    86→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℕ`, `ℤ`, `∀`, `∈`, `∧`, `↔`, `∃`, `≤`.
    87→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    88→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    89→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~80 characters).
    90→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    91→
    92→## 12. Build Verification
    93→
    94→- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review.
    95→- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified as part of this review.
    96→
    97→---
    98→
    99→## Summary
   100→
   101→**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)
   102→
   103→The file is well-formed and conforms to all applicable checklist items. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, custom definitions (`IsSidonSet`, `DisjointDifferences`), theorem statement, `answer()` usage (Case 1 — yes/no question), category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality all meet the required standards.
   104→
   105→The formalization correctly models the "is it true that" question using `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, with `S` universally quantified and constrained to be a maximum-size Sidon set in `{1, …, N}`, and an existential constant `C` capturing the `O(1)` term. The only note is that the second part of the source problem (equal-size variant) is not formalized, which could be addressed with a variant in the future.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/43.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 43`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/43`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem.
- [x] Bibliography entry present: [Er95] with full citation.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos43 ... end Erdos43`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos43`).
- [x] `open Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 32).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `IsSidonSet` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `DisjointDifferences` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- N/A: No noncomputable operations (pure logical predicates).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `IsSidonSet`, `DisjointDifferences`.
- N/A: No functions returning types to check for `lowerCamelCase`.
- N/A: No problem-specific `local notation` needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]`/`@[category API]` sanity checks added (optional "consider" item).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_43` (snake_case, matching namespace number 43).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source paper: [Er95].

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks "is it true that ..." — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked.
- [x] Problem is open, so `answer(sorry)` (not `answer(True)`/`answer(False)`) is correct.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked: `∃ C : ℕ, ∀ (N : ℕ) (A B S : Finset ℕ), [Sidon & range conditions] → [S is maximal] → DisjointDifferences A B → Nat.choose A.card 2 + Nat.choose B.card 2 ≤ Nat.choose S.card 2 + C`. Thus `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no".
- [x] Quantifiers (`∃ C`, `∀ N A B S`) go **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body — consistent.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present: `AMS 5 11`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 11).
- [x] Numbers in ascending numeric order: 5 < 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both `5` (combinatorics) and `11` (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses multi-line `by\n  sorry` format (lines 68–69), which is acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not in `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. Only the main theorem is declared.
- **Note:** The source problem on erdosproblems.com includes a second part asking whether the bound can be improved when |A| = |B|. This second part is not formalized. A variant `erdos_43.variants.equal_size` could be added but is not required.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℕ`, `ℤ`, `∀`, `∈`, `∧`, `↔`, `∃`, `≤`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (longest line ~80 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review.
- [ ] Custom linter pass not verified as part of this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)

The file is well-formed and conforms to all applicable checklist items. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace, custom definitions (`IsSidonSet`, `DisjointDifferences`), theorem statement, `answer()` usage (Case 1 — yes/no question), category/AMS attributes, proof body, and code quality all meet the required standards.

The formalization correctly models the "is it true that" question using `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, with `S` universally quantified and constrained to be a maximum-size Sidon set in `{1, …, N}`, and an existential constant `C` capturing the `O(1)` term. The only note is that the second part of the source problem (equal-size variant) is not formalized, which could be addressed with a variant in the future.

content.text: The review result is **PASS**. No changes needed.
